metamorphicTransforms.intent: Guard memory.size != 0, compute the last byte address from memory.size * 65536 - 1, restore that byte with a reachable length-1 memory.fill using the currently loaded byte value, then byte load/drop it. Uses i32 operands for memory32 and i64 operands for memory64-only modules, preserving source-visible memory while exercising live nonzero nonzero-address fill/read behavior.

rows.notes: pass-oi-default-scalar now emits three deterministic seed-indexed trigger-smoke cases for direct, local-carried, and local.tee/commuted OI-D shapes instead of one fixed module. The count-3 grouped OI-D sweep reaches comparison and reports three raw mismatches with no validation/generator/property/command failures; classify them as open scalar parity evidence until implemented or measured, not automatic semantic-safe drift. Future work should replace the seed-indexed smoke selector with randomized trigger-biased scalar constructors and wrappers around existing producers. The sweep runner prints result summaries (and can load them with --summarize-existing), so future OI-D/OI-E triage should start from profile-case and case-label status groups before opening individual WAT diffs. A 2026-06-27 representative diff pass classified the three trigger labels in classifiedCaseLabels. The narrow literal double-eqz final-folding candidate is now implemented and covered. The fresh count-3 OI-D sweep shows residual mismatches only from Starshine extra folds of constant compare and/or constant sign-extension shapes; those sampled residuals are measured smaller Starshine outputs (-5/-2/-2 canonical wasm bytes) and all outputs validate, but the row remains open until broader randomized trigger coverage and/or source-backed family policy decides whether to retain or align these output-shape wins.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: oi-default-scalar:local-tee-commuted-add-zero
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: measured size-winning Starshine output drift over pure constant compare and constant sign-extension folds
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: Binaryen keeps one pure constant i32.eq and explicit constant sign-extension opcodes; Starshine folds both constant i32.eq forms and direct constant sign extensions to constants. After the literal double-eqz fix both sides agree on i32.const 1. The sampled Starshine canonical wasm is 91 bytes vs Binaryen 96 bytes (-5 bytes), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a measured Starshine-win candidate unless broader randomized OI-D sweeps expose a semantic or size regression; otherwise source-back this constant-folding policy or align to Binaryen if the project decides output-shape parity is preferred.
classifiedCaseLabels.lastCheckedEvidence: 2026-06-27 fresh OI-D count-3 sweep at .tmp/oi-parity-sweep-oi-d-double-eqz-fix-smoke; stat measured canonical wasm byte deltas; wasm-tools validate --features all passed for Binaryen and Starshine canonical outputs.

rows.title: Memory/load/store and bulk-memory tiny-lowering boundaries
rows.binaryenOracleMode: compare-pass normalized WAT with all-features; focused rows must keep trap-relaxed zero-size mode separate
rows.blockerBoundaryReason: The OI-G trigger profile has twenty-seven seed-indexed/BodyStream-randomized trigger-smoke-plus cases, adding source-visible shared-memory seed-derived aligned-address full-width atomic xchg restore coverage to the prior runtime store8-mask, full-width xchg, shared wait/notify, runtime restore/fill/call/localtee/select, memory64, multi-memory, and tiny bulk surfaces. The latest grouped count405 runtime run compares 405/405 with 333 normalized matches, 72 mismatches, zero validation/generator/property/command failures, and runtime checked/unsupported/failed 185/220/0. The new shared-atomic-random-xchg label samples 11/11 residuals; inspected representatives preserve the same atomic load/xchg restore contract, runtime-supported samples are equal, and the residual is Binaryen choosing folded i32.const address spelling for one i64 atomic load where Starshine keeps offset=8 on the seed-derived base. Residual buckets are all Starshine smaller or canonical-equal except WAT text increases in atomic offset-folding buckets. OI-G remains open for broader grouped mismatch reduction, randomized atomic RMW/cmpxchg beyond xchg, true shared-memory ordering/concurrency, broader wait/notify including blocking behavior, broader source-visible mutation/restore shapes, true live trap/effect ordering, broader store/address commutation, randomized memory64/multi-memory wrappers, raw/text residual policy, and exact cross-memory/runtime execution where needed. OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT remains blocked until true descriptor-compatible evidence exists; this non-descriptor OI-G evidence must not close it.

implementationNotes: 2026-06-27: oi-memory-size-boundary is implemented as a smoke metamorphic transform over dead memory.copy/memory.fill tiny-size variants for i32 memories; row remains mismatch pending live boundary, memory64, trigger-biased generator, and executed sweep evidence.

2026-06-27: oi-commuted-operands is implemented as a smoke metamorphic transform over synthetic i32 constant add/and/eq operand-order pairs; row remains mismatch pending memory-address/store-mask trigger wrappers and live memory boundary variants.

2026-06-27: oi-effectful-sibling is implemented as a smoke metamorphic transform over a private mutable i32 global and stack-neutral effectful shells; row remains mismatch pending live memory/bulk wrappers, memory64, dynamic operands, and trapping sibling variants.

2026-06-28: red-first dead-if/raw-skip coverage now lowers size 1/2/4/8/16 memory.copy/fill in dead synthetic shells while preserving zero-size and size-3/5 bulk spellings; the sampled memory-size-boundary raw residual is now classified as structural/code-section drift from extra load/store/atomic offset folding plus one fewer local, while OI-G remains open on broader memory64/dynamic/live effect/trap surfaces and raw-byte signoff alignment if required.

2026-06-28: pass-oi-memory-bulk is now a seed-indexed trigger-smoke profile with direct i32 tiny-bulk/load-store, memory64 dynamic/local-carried bulk, and live private-global plus dead-trap boundary cases. The direct count-3 lane normalized-matches all three profile cases; the grouped count-5 transform lane leaves three classified output-shape/cross-family residuals and keeps OI-G open for randomized memory wrappers, live ordering, atomics/store masks, and broader memory64/dynamic evidence.

2026-06-28: pass-oi-memory-bulk expanded from three to six trigger-smoke-plus cases with existing-producer/no-param-call wrapped bulk, store-mask, and dynamic i32 local.tee bulk operands. Direct count-18 samples all six labels; grouped count-6 exposes a size-losing store-mask + dead memory-size-boundary residual and a count>=11 generator/metamorphic command failure when memory-size-boundary reaches a later memory64 case, so OI-G remains open.

2026-06-28: red-first raw partial coverage now drops redundant i32/i64 low-bit masks before narrow stores after a dead memory-size-boundary-like shell. The grouped store-mask residual is no longer the prior size-losing canonical parity gap; it is reduced to the same low-byte-store Starshine-win candidate as the direct lane, with a remaining raw-size residual and OI-G still open.

2026-06-28: red-first raw partial coverage now rewrites adjacent nonnegative signed div/rem-by-zero trap spellings to Binaryen's unsigned forms in raw-skipped optimize-instructions functions. This removes the sampled dead scalar trap spelling residual from the grouped OI-G live-effect and multi-memory labels; it is a spelling/parity reduction, not live OI-G trap-order closure.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-zero-memory-boundary, a reachable zero-size memory.copy/fill smoke transform for memory32 and memory64-only modules. This broadens OI-G grouped evidence toward live no-op bulk-memory boundaries but is not live trap-order or atomics closure.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-zero-memory-boundary, a reachable zero-size memory.copy/fill smoke transform for memory32 and memory64-only modules. Grouped count-70 samples 11 live-zero cases and all 11 match; this broadens live no-op bulk-memory evidence but is not live trap-order, atomics, or randomized memory closure.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-copy-boundary, a guarded reachable length-1 memory.copy-from-self plus byte load/drop smoke transform. This broadens OI-G beyond zero-size live no-ops into live nonzero copy/read evidence, but still does not cover memory.fill restore wrappers, atomics, randomized addresses, or true live trap ordering.

2026-06-28: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-copy-boundary, a guarded reachable length-1 memory.copy-from-self plus byte load/drop smoke transform. Its first grouped run exposed a raw-skip i32.ne(memory.size, 0) if-condition spelling residual; a red-first raw partial fix now matches Binaryen's memory.size condition spelling. The post-fix grouped count-77 leaves only direct-tiny offset-folding/raw residuals under this transform, with zero canonical size-losing failure dirs.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: transform:oi-memory-size-boundary

classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The transform prepends dead memory.copy/memory.fill variants for sizes 0/1/2/3/4/5/8/16. After fix4, Binaryen and Starshine both lower sampled size 1/2/4/8/16 variants and both leave 10 memory.copy plus 10 memory.fill operations; v128.load/store counts are 2/4 on both sides. The WAT diff is dominated by Starshine folding constant ordinary/atomic memory addresses into immediates where Binaryen keeps nonzero i32.const addresses (186 offset-bearing memory ops vs Binaryen 104) plus one fewer function-0 i32 local. Section dumps localize all measured size deltas to code: raw code Starshine/Binaryen 6162/6100 bytes, canonical code 6080/6108. Raw wasm is Starshine/Binaryen 6291/6229 bytes, canonical wasm is 6209/6237 bytes, and all raw/canonical outputs validate. A later count-110 after raw constant memory access offset folding samples memory-size-boundary ten times with eight matches and two remaining store-mask raw-size residual mismatches; the old direct-tiny/memory64 offset-folding residuals are gone.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Keep as a sampled candidate only for the synthetic dead-shell contract. Reopen as an OI-G parity gap if memory opcode counts drift again, if live effect/trap ordering or dynamic/memory64 operands fail, if canonical size regresses, or if a raw-byte signoff lane decides to align the extra offset-folding/local-section structure.

2026-06-29: red-first metamorphic coverage added oi-live-nonzero-memory-atomic-wait-notify-boundary, a private shared-memory nonblocking wait/notify transform. It appends a private shared i32 memory with min=max=1, guards memory.size != 0, emits memory.atomic.notify, memory.atomic.wait32, and memory.atomic.wait64, and chooses wait expected values that differ from zero-initialized private memory with timeout 0 so the waits do not block and source-visible memories are not mutated. Grouped count350 at .tmp/oi-g-atomic-wait-notify-count350-20260629 compared 182/350 transform-applicable cases with 160 normalized matches, 22 mismatches, zero validation/generator/property/command failures, and the new transform 9/9 matches. Manual failure-dir review found zero validation failures and zero raw/canonical/normalized-WAT Starshine-larger dirs. This broadens wait/notify evidence only; it does not close true shared-memory ordering, source-visible shared-memory effects, blocking wait behavior, full-width/source-visible xchg, OI-G, or OI-J descriptor/exactness/TNH/IIT.

This broadens atomic-load evidence only; later non-primary/private atomic RMW/cmpxchg smoke covers the non-primary fixed/dynamic no-op subset, while OI-G remains open for randomized atomic addresses, xchg, wait/notify, true shared-memory ordering, true live trap/effect ordering, grouped mismatch reduction, and raw-size policy.

All three sampled transformed outputs have 0 Starshine call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get residuals, matching Binaryen, so this sweep does not expose a remaining sampled OI-H target-spelling gap. The remaining raw mismatches are local-section/dropped-ref.func shape drift in the target-wrapper sample and cross-family local-section/dropped-null/effectful-sibling scalar/if-shape drift in the local-carried/effectful samples. Broader true live table-state known-target, return_call_ref, argument-localization, multivalue, and effect/trap target-boundary sweeps remain open.

rows.notes: oi-call-ref-target-wrapper is implemented-smoke and forwarded by the sweep runner: it appends a private no-op target and prepends direct ref.func/call_ref, block-returned ref.func/call_ref, typed-select target/call_ref, and dead call_ref shells to existing defined functions. A default GenValid one-case compare produced one raw mismatch with no validation/generator/command failures; the earlier pass-oi-call-ref seed-0x5eed validity blocker from stringref/exn ref pools is fixed and the profile-backed smoke now reaches comparison. The profile-backed representative first exposed a large size-losing OI-H parity gap where Binaryen directized synthetic ref.func call_ref wrappers while Starshine left call_ref/ref.func spelling. The raw skip path now directizes adjacent ref.func/block-ref.func, constant-select ref.func, and single-use local.set(ref.func)/local.get call_ref/return_call_ref wrappers, and it trims guaranteed null-as-non-null, dropped-unreachable-block, dropped null ref.test/ref.cast, and dropped non-null ref.cast trap tails before returning a skipped function, without widening the full HOT raw gate. The latest one-case target-wrapper smoke still mismatches, but OI-H call target spelling is no longer the sampled residual: Starshine and Binaryen both have 0 call_ref/table.get residuals, 20 direct `call $5` occurrences, 0 ref.test/ref.cast/br_on_cast/br_on_cast_fail, and five `unreachable` roots. The remaining 18-byte raw size gap is local-section/ref.func-drop output shape drift: Starshine drops the inserted `ref.func` uses while Binaryen keeps five dropped `ref.func` instructions. A follow-up count-3 grouped OI-H sweep at `.tmp/oi-h-target-wrapper-grouped-sweep-20260627` mixed the target-wrapper, local-carried, and effectful-sibling transforms; all three cases still mismatched, but every sampled Starshine and Binaryen output had call_ref=0, return_call_ref=0, and table.get=0. The target-wrapper case remains the local-section/ref.func-drop shape drift; the local-carried case is local-section/dropped-null shape drift after successful call_ref cleanup; the effectful-sibling case is cross-family effectful private-global if/scalar spelling drift after successful call_ref cleanup. A 2026-06-30 measurement validates all grouped raw/canonical artifacts and shows the residual set is raw-size-losing overall (Starshine raw +129 bytes) while canonical and WAT are smaller (-72/-3473); keep the residual classified as output-shape/cross-family drift, not broad OI-H closure. Future OI-H work should run broader table/local/return_call_ref/multivalue sweeps to find true remaining call_ref directization gaps instead of over-mining these cross-family residuals. A timeout-conscious count6 refresh at `.tmp/oi-h-focused-count6-20260630` avoided the earlier larger-run timeout and sampled two cases for each forwarded transform. It did not expose new call_ref/return_call_ref/table.get residuals: all sampled outputs stayed target-clean on both sides. The residual remains raw-size-losing in aggregate while canonical and WAT are smaller, so OI-H stays open for broader target-generation rather than another fix to the already-clean sampled wrappers.

rows.notes: OI-M has extensive one-off boundary research through many effect-count rows. The family workflow now provides forwarded selected-lane, effectful-sibling, dead trap-shaped sibling, and local-carried smoke transforms, and now has trigger-biased tuple-profile constructors including helper-call-produced selected-lane, local.tee-produced selected-lane, select-produced selected-lane, selected trapping lane, selected effectful lane, first live effectful non-selected sibling smoke, first live trapping non-selected sibling smoke, and first effectful select-operand selected-lane smoke, first trapping select-operand selected-lane smoke, and first multi-selected effectful i64 lane smoke, but still needs broader existing-producer/effect/trap selected-lane wrappers beyond the current smoke cases, tuple-scratch localization constructors, runtime semantic execution where relevant, and randomized multivalue breadth to stop producing one boundary doc per lane/effect count. The first synthetic `oi-tuple-selected-lane` smoke mismatch is classified in `classifiedCaseLabels`: for pure constant multivalue block lane extraction, Starshine keeps nested block-expression localization while Binaryen scalarizes to more straight-line local sets; the sampled Starshine canonical/raw wasm is smaller and both outputs validate, so this is a sampled Starshine-win candidate, not a closure of the broader tuple-scratch reconstruction family.

classifiedCaseLabels.label: pass-oi-tuple:oi-tuple-selected-lane-smoke
classifiedCaseLabels.agentClassification: sampled size-winning representation drift over pure synthetic multivalue selected-lane smoke; broader OI-M tuple-scratch localization remains open
classifiedCaseLabels.observedDiff: The transform prepends pure constant multivalue block selected-lane shells. Binaryen scalarizes the shell into many straight-line local.set/local.tee operations; Starshine keeps nested block-result expressions around the selected lane. Starshine canonical wasm is 4100 bytes vs Binaryen 4326 bytes (-226), raw wasm is 3975 vs 4229 bytes (-254), and both canonical outputs validate.
classifiedCaseLabels.nextAction: Do not treat this smoke as OI-M signoff. Upgrade pass-oi-tuple / oi-tuple-selected-lane to wrap existing tuple producers with effectful/trapping siblings and selected-lane variants; preserve this sample as a Starshine-win candidate unless broader probes show semantic or size regressions.
